world_date: Month of the Ashen Dreaming, Year 1 of the Hollow Accord, Day 20
major_event: Ironmaul received no word of Ashveil's escalation letter — but Varek spoke a name this morning that no one in the garrison had ever told him. In the Twin Cities, Caeris dispatched his High Cleric inquiry and received a preliminary answer. In Tidefall, the Admiral's two-day window is half-spent. Rowen has not emerged from his cabin. The world holds its breath one more day, and the pressure mounts beneath every surface.

primary_event.name: Varek Speaks the Name — Entity Communication Deepens
primary_event.summary: At dawn, Varek addressed Ironmaul directly for the first time in three days without being spoken to first. He said a name: Ashveil had referred to no one by name in his presence since arriving, yet Varek said the name of the Warden she had written to — Aldric — without context or explanation. Ironmaul asked how he knew. Varek looked at the iron case. Kessa Stonebraid's morning rune-check showed the case surface had developed faint etchings overnight, not carved from without but formed from within — three spiral marks, new and precise. Ashveil, observing from the corner, went still. She recognized the marks. They appear in a Monastery text called the Concordance of Sealed Thresholds — a pre-institutional record considered too dangerous for general circulation. No one in Lostfeld should know that text exists.

supporting_events.name: Rowen — The Map's Second Point Identified
supporting_events.summary: Rowen emerged from his cabin before dawn, alone, and spent the morning hours cross-referencing the cloth map against his fleet navigator's sealed charts without telling the navigator why. By midmorning he had tentatively identified a second marked point: a coastal shelf northeast of the Tidefall approaches, known but rarely visited, historically associated with old wrecks. The third, fourth, and fifth points remain unidentified. He re-sealed the map and returned to his cabin. The fleet crew is growing restless — three sailors asked the quartermaster if they were leaving today. The quartermaster had no answer.

supporting_events.name: Caeris — The Clergy Answers, The Clock Tightens
supporting_events.summary: The High Cleric of the Twin Cities sent a reply to Caeris's inquiry by midday: he would witness a proclamation under the contingency clause, but required the text to be submitted for a twenty-four-hour review before signing. Lady Orryn sent a note at the same hour: she would give her answer at tomorrow morning's meeting. Lord Halveth arrived unannounced in the afternoon to ask if the clergy witness had been secured — he is growing anxious, does not want to be the only name on a failed document. Caeris told him: by tomorrow evening. He now has a hard deadline he created himself.

supporting_events.name: Admiral's Clock — The Prophet Moves
supporting_events.summary: The Admiral did not emerge on Day 20 and submitted no written statement. One day remains before the incapacitation protocol triggers. The prophet, agitated since the previous evening's notification, gained access to the sealed study antechamber at midday — the junior clerk, unnerved, did not stop her. She stood at the closed door and spoke through it for approximately twenty minutes. No audible response came from within. She emerged and told the clerk only: 'He is present.' The councillors received this secondhand and debated whether 'present' constitutes a written statement under the protocol language.

active_events.name: Varek and the Iron Case — Entity Speaks Through the Sealed
active_events.summary: Varek named Warden Aldric without prompting. New spiral etchings formed on the case surface overnight — from within. Ashveil recognized them as symbols from the Concordance of Sealed Thresholds, a restricted pre-institutional Monastery text. Ironmaul does not know this text exists. Ashveil's escalation letter is en route to Stonebreak via the Gnome relay but has not yet arrived. The entity appears to be actively communicating — not through Varek alone, but through the case itself.
active_events.consequences: If the etchings continue, the case becomes a readable document. If Ironmaul learns the text's significance before Stonebreak responds, he may act unilaterally. Ashveil is now holding information she has not shared with Ironmaul — the Concordance recognition — and that concealment is a gamble. Varek is the only channel of direct communication and he may not be fully in control of what passes through him.

active_events.name: Rowen — Three Objects from the Deep Chamber
active_events.summary: Rowen has identified two of the five map points. The cloth map is becoming a navigation document rather than an artifact. Fleet crew restlessness is rising — they have been at anchor with no orders for two days. Rowen has not shared the map's significance with anyone but has used the fleet navigator's charts without explanation. The navigator suspects something unusual.
active_events.consequences: Rowen cannot stay at anchor indefinitely without fleet morale degrading. He must either move toward one of the identified map points, return to familiar waters, or reveal enough of the situation to justify continued stillness. Every day of silence increases the chance a crew member asks the wrong question aloud.

active_events.name: The Recall Standoff — Contract Review Holds the Camp
active_events.summary: The Dur Khadur unit continues its contractual hold at four miles from the Twin Cities. The contract-review result dispatch sits unread on the Tidefall admiralty desk alongside two other unread documents. Caeris now has the clergy witness committed and is one day from Lady Orryn's answer — if both come through, he will be in a position to issue a formal stand-down as regent-designate. The unit commander has received no new instructions from any party.
active_events.consequences: The standoff resolves only when a legitimate authority issues a formal stand-down to Dur Khadur. Caeris is the most likely actor but is not yet in formal authority. If the proclamation is announced before the stand-down, Dur Khadur will be the first external test of whether the new authority is real.

active_events.name: The Proclamation Crisis — One Day to Decision
active_events.summary: The High Cleric requires a twenty-four-hour text review. Lady Orryn gives her answer tomorrow morning. Lord Halveth is anxious and checking in early. Caeris has set himself a hard deadline of tomorrow evening. The king did not wake today — the physician's continued silence is the loudest absence. If Caeris has all three signatures by tomorrow evening, the proclamation can proceed.
active_events.consequences: Tomorrow is the decisive day for the succession announcement. If Orryn declines, Caeris must find a third noble — or proceed without her, which weakens the document. If the clergy review finds doctrinal problems with the contingency clause language, the timeline collapses. The Glenhaven Wayfarer's two-day reply window also expires tomorrow.

active_events.name: Shadow Court — Lythara's Envoy Rides for Arkenor
active_events.summary: Lythara's personal envoy departed for Arkenor's estate before dawn with the full Tidefall intelligence package and a private offer Lythara composed at midnight. Velorn is aware Lythara moved quickly — one of his watchers noted the envoy's departure — and dispatched his own representative toward Arkenor by midmorning, approximately six hours behind Lythara's envoy. Arkenor will receive two competing delegations within one to two days.
active_events.consequences: Whoever arrives at Arkenor first with the more compelling offer shapes the Shadow Court's near future. Lythara has better intelligence but Velorn has the council-record advantage and a fresher public narrative. Arkenor is not neutral — he has interests — and both sides may be underestimating what he actually wants.

active_events.name: The Admiral's Sealed Study — Incapacitation Clock Final Day
active_events.summary: The Admiral has not emerged and has given no written statement. One day remains. The prophet's visit produced the ambiguous word 'present' which the councillors are debating. The Vampire operative is watching the council debate and has sent a preliminary update to Lythara's network. The unread Dur Khadur contract dispatch on the admiralty desk may become a crisis if the incapacitation protocol triggers — the new acting authority will inherit all unread documents.
active_events.consequences: If the protocol triggers tomorrow, Tidefall's admiralty passes to the council chair, who will open the desk documents. The Dur Khadur dispatch and Caeris's Hollow Accord letter will then both be read within hours of power transfer. The situation could shift dramatically depending on who reads them first and what they conclude.

active_events.name: Vilefin Tinker — Intelligence Sale at Farrock
active_events.summary: The tinker-informant met Farrock's garrison intelligence officer this morning. He described what he witnessed at Lostfeld: the sound Varek made at night — rhythmic, non-verbal, not distress — and the smell of cold iron beneath the garrison floor, distinct from normal forge-smell. The intelligence officer paid a modest sum and recorded both details. The garrison commander was briefed by afternoon. The Day 16 signal gap in the northern corridor now has a partial explanation.
active_events.consequences: Farrock now has fragmentary but genuine intelligence about something unusual at Lostfeld — not enough to act on but enough to make them more anxious. If they share this with the Twin Cities, Caeris gains another piece of the picture. If they sit on it, Farrock's isolation grows. The tinker is preparing to depart tomorrow morning — possibly toward the Gnome pass.

active_events.name: Glenhaven Intelligence — Wayfarer's Reply Window
active_events.summary: The Wayfarer's two-day reply window expires tomorrow. Elowen's two standby Wayfarers remain at the Twin Cities border. Elowen has received no update and will not send a follow-up — she considers premature pressure a sign of weakness. Caeris has not yet drafted his formal reply; he added it to tomorrow's task list alongside Lady Orryn's answer and the clergy review.
active_events.consequences: Tomorrow Caeris must either accept Glenhaven's facilitation offer, decline it, or request an extension. An extension would reveal that he is overloaded with competing pressures — which is information Elowen would use. A rejection would close off the northern intelligence network at a moment when Lostfeld is showing unusual activity.

faction_actions.action: Ironmaul convened a private meeting with Kessa Stonebraid and two senior rune-wardens to examine the overnight etchings without Ashveil present. He ordered detailed rubbings made of all three spiral marks before they could change further. He did not inform Ashveil of the private session. He sent a preliminary report to the Thane's Council — not about the entity, but about the Monastery's jurisdictional claim — framing it as an institutional encroachment requiring clarification.
faction_actions.reason: Ironmaul is establishing a formal record of his position before Ashveil's escalation letter arrives. He suspects she has escalated; he does not yet know she recognized the etchings. He is fortifying his institutional standing in anticipation of the Thane's Council being drawn in.
faction_actions.target: Rune-wardens — assigned to document etchings; Thane's Council — jurisdictional report dispatched; Ashveil — excluded from morning session

faction_actions.action: Ashveil spent the morning in controlled silence while observing the iron case. When Ironmaul's rune-wardens arrived for the rubbing session and she was excluded, she did not protest — she walked to the garrison's outer yard, sat on a stone bench, and wrote a second short letter to Stonebreak, this one marked urgent-eyes-only for Warden Aldric: 'The case is producing Concordance marks. Do not relay this through the pass station. Send a courier by the southern road.' She sealed it herself and paid a Lostfeld civilian to carry it.
faction_actions.reason: Ashveil has realized the Gnome relay may be monitored or simply too slow. She is sending a second channel using a non-Gnome courier, and she is withholding the Concordance detail from everyone in Lostfeld including Ironmaul. She knows the marks and knows their significance — and she is not ready to share that leverage.
faction_actions.target: Warden Aldric — second urgent letter dispatched via civilian courier; Concordance information — withheld from Ironmaul; Gnome relay — deliberately bypassed

faction_actions.action: The Dark Council received an internal report from the unit commander: camp morale is holding but supply pressure is beginning to build at day seven of the hold. The Council authorized a resupply caravan — three wagons from the nearest Dur Khadur depot — to maintain the unit's capacity to hold for another fourteen days if required. They also sent a brief formal note to Caeris's office: the unit will stand down upon receipt of a formal written order from the Twin Cities regent-designate office. One sentence. No elaboration.
faction_actions.reason: Dur Khadur is signaling to Caeris that they are willing to recognize his authority — they just need him to formally exercise it. This is partly commercial (it resolves their contractual ambiguity) and partly strategic (it gives them a relationship with whoever wins the succession).
faction_actions.target: Unit camp — resupply caravan dispatched; Caeris — formal stand-down pathway communicated; unit commander — fourteen-day supply window confirmed

faction_actions.action: The senior trade councillor called a formal session to debate the prophet's 'he is present' report. After two hours the session voted: the verbal relay from the prophet does not constitute a written statement under the protocol's plain language. The Admiral has until dawn tomorrow to appear or submit writing. The clerk was instructed to post the deadline formally on the admiralty door. Two additional councillors joined the discussion — one argued for leniency, one for immediate incapacitation. The vote was narrow.
faction_actions.reason: The council is not unanimous and the narrow vote reveals the political fracture. If the incapacitation protocol triggers, the council chair becomes acting authority — but several councillors are unsure the chair has the backbone to handle the Dur Khadur dispatch and the Hollow Accord simultaneously.
faction_actions.target: Admiral — formal deadline posted on door; council — narrow vote recorded; prophet — informed of ruling, given no formal role in protocol

faction_actions.action: Caeris submitted the proclamation text to the High Cleric for review. He then prepared three separate letters: a formal acceptance of Glenhaven's facilitation offer (conditional on confidentiality terms), a brief acknowledgment to Dur Khadur's note confirming he would issue a formal stand-down once the proclamation was announced, and a short message to Lord Halveth assuring him the clergy track was active. He sent all three before midday and then cleared his afternoon for whatever Lady Orryn sends tomorrow.
faction_actions.reason: Caeris is executing his decision map from last night. He has chosen to accept Glenhaven, signal to Dur Khadur, and manage Halveth's anxiety — all before Lady Orryn's answer arrives. If Orryn declines, he has already activated Glenhaven as an alternative legitimacy channel.
faction_actions.target: High Cleric — proclamation text submitted for review; Glenhaven Wayfarer — conditional acceptance dispatched; Dur Khadur — stand-down acknowledgment sent; Lord Halveth — reassurance note delivered

faction_actions.action: Lythara's envoy is en route to Arkenor — expected to arrive by tomorrow morning given the distance through Faerwood's inner roads. Lythara spent the day in her chambers reviewing what she knows about Arkenor's personal interests: he controls three of the Court's best glass trade routes through the Gnome passes and has been quietly profitable during the Court's recent instability. She revised her offer accordingly — instead of political alliance, she is offering Arkenor a seat on the intelligence council and formal protection of his Gnome-pass trade arrangements.
faction_actions.reason: Lythara is approaching Arkenor as a commercial actor, not a political one. She suspects Velorn is offering political elevation — which Arkenor may distrust. A commercial guarantee with intelligence access is harder to walk away from.
faction_actions.target: Arkenor — revised offer prepared for envoy to deliver; Gnome-pass trade — promised protection as leverage; Velorn — competing offer now six hours behind

faction_actions.action: The Gnome relay station completed its internal review of the Sinking Island anchorage request. Conclusion: the requesting party used a routing cipher last seen in Tidefall's admiralty correspondence three years ago — before the current Admiral's tenure. The request was not from the current Admiral's office. It was filed under a secondary account. The Gnomes flagged this to their syndicate council and declined to fulfill the request, but retained the record. They also logged that Ashveil's urgent-eyes-only letter had been bypassed — a civilian courier had been used instead, which is a protocol violation they noted but did not act on.
faction_actions.reason: The anchorage request is now a minor intelligence asset — someone in or near Tidefall's old admiralty network is tracking Rowen's position. The Gnomes will not share this without payment, but they now have a piece of information that multiple parties would value.
faction_actions.target: Sinking Island anchorage request — declined, origin flagged as old Tidefall cipher; Ashveil bypass — noted for record; syndicate council — briefed on anomaly

faction_actions.action: Elowen received Caeris's conditional acceptance at midday — faster than she expected, which told her he was moving urgently. She accepted the confidentiality terms without modification and dispatched the two standby Wayfarers to formally enter Twin Cities territory as observers. She also composed a private intelligence summary for her own records: the Lostfeld situation involves the Monastery, which means the Gnome pass is adjacent to an escalating institutional conflict. She sent a quiet inquiry to the Gnome pass station asking for any relevant route-condition updates.
faction_actions.reason: Elowen is threading her embedding into the Twin Cities succession while simultaneously monitoring the northern situation. She does not yet know about the Concordance marks — but she knows Ashveil went to Lostfeld and that the situation was serious enough to require a Monastery override.
faction_actions.target: Twin Cities — two Wayfarers formally deployed as observers; Glenhaven-Twin Cities facilitation — now active; Gnome pass — route inquiry sent

faction_actions.action: Rowen called a full officers' meeting at midday — the first since the descent. He told them the submerged structure was a pre-ruin repository, that he had recovered navigational materials, and that they would be setting course within forty-eight hours. He did not specify the destination. He asked the navigator to prepare route options for three heading possibilities — northeast, south-southeast, and due south — without explaining why. The navigator complied with visible curiosity. The crew, hearing that movement was coming, visibly steadied.
faction_actions.reason: Rowen has calculated that crew morale required something — a promise of movement without a revelation of destination. He is buying himself two days to identify the remaining map points while maintaining the fleet's operational cohesion.
faction_actions.target: Officers — briefed in minimal terms; navigator — route options for three headings requested; crew morale — stabilized with movement promise; fleet — remains at anchor for 48 more hours

faction_actions.action: The tinker-informant collected his payment from Farrock's intelligence officer and spent the afternoon in the merchant bunk preparing to depart. He wrote a brief coded note to Vilefin intelligence chief Skrix: 'Farrock is anxious and buying. Lostfeld details sold for twelve silver. Gnome pass next — may have something for Stonebreak route.' He sealed it and left it with the Farrock gate courier outbox, addressed through a Vilefin relay point.
faction_actions.reason: The tinker is a professional. He has completed the Farrock sale and is now calculating his next market. The Gnome pass is his route home and Stonebreak is adjacent — if the Lostfeld situation is escalating, Stonebreak may be willing to pay for detail.
faction_actions.target: Skrix — coded status report sent; Farrock sale — completed; Gnome pass — next destination; Stonebreak — potential secondary buyer being considered

faction_actions.action: The garrison commander reviewed the tinker's intelligence and correlated it with the Day 16 signal gap. He sent a formal written inquiry to the Twin Cities requesting clarification on the Lostfeld situation — specifically whether the Twin Cities had received any reports of unusual activity in the northern corridor. He addressed it to the regent-designate office. He also ordered the northern watchtower crew to increase their signal frequency to every four hours.
faction_actions.reason: Farrock's self-interest is direct: if something is wrong in Lostfeld, it is in Farrock's approaches. The Twin Cities inquiry is the most legitimate channel available to them — and it will land on Caeris's desk at an already-busy moment.
faction_actions.target: Twin Cities regent-designate office — formal inquiry dispatched; northern watchtowers — signal frequency increased; garrison — on heightened northern alert

faction_actions.action: The operative spent the day pursuing the document's true origin. Working through two intermediaries, they traced the external courier's arrival point to a harbor-side inn frequented by Tidefall's independent trader captains. The innkeeper remembered a figure in a grey traveling cloak who had left a package three days before the Admiral sealed his study. The figure had not given a name. The operative now has a physical description — tall, grey-cloaked, carried no visible weapons — and a date. They sent this to Lythara's network with the note: 'Origin is individual, not institutional. Not Shadow Court origin.'
faction_actions.reason: The Vampires are narrowing the document's origin. Ruling out the Shadow Court is significant — it means neither Lythara nor Velorn sent it, which makes the actor unknown and potentially more dangerous. The tall grey-cloaked figure is a lead.
faction_actions.target: Document origin — narrowed to individual courier, not institutional; Lythara network — physical description and ruling-out transmitted; grey-cloaked figure — active lead

faction_actions.action: The test caravan departed at dawn via the northern bypass toward Frostvale. By midday a relay rider returned with a report: the first Rock Plains segment is passable but the opioid-dependent crew chiefs are showing increased instability on the unfamiliar road — two altercations at the morning rest stop. The elder council received the report with concern but did not recall the caravan. They authorized a single escort of four armed riders to accompany the crew.
faction_actions.reason: The council is committed to the test route and unwilling to pull back before they have data. The instability is expected and manageable at this scale. If the caravan reaches Frostvale, they have proven the bypass works. If it fails, they learn the price of the addiction problem on unfamiliar terrain.
faction_actions.target: Test caravan — en route, northern bypass; Rock Plains segment — passable but unstable; four armed riders — dispatched as escort; opioid instability — deferred, monitored

faction_actions.action: After dispatching his four midday letters, Caeris received the Farrock inquiry by late afternoon. He added it to his stack, wrote a marginal note — 'northern corridor unusual activity, correlate with Dur Khadur camp position?' — and placed it aside for tomorrow. He does not yet know about the Lostfeld entity. He only knows that Farrock is nervous and that something moved through the northern signal chain without announcing itself.
faction_actions.reason: Caeris is approaching information saturation. He is correctly noting the Farrock inquiry as potentially significant but lacks the context to understand it. Tomorrow's agenda — Orryn's answer, clergy review, Glenhaven deployment, Dur Khadur stand-down, and now this — is already overwhelming one man.
faction_actions.target: Farrock inquiry — received, noted, deferred to tomorrow; northern corridor — flagged but unresolved; strategy stack — growing faster than resolution rate

recent_events.text: At dawn, Varek named Warden Aldric without prompting — a name no one in the garrison had spoken in his presence. Kessa Stonebraid's morning rune-check found three spiral etchings on the case surface, formed from within overnight. Ashveil recognized them as Concordance of Sealed Thresholds marks but said nothing to Ironmaul. Ironmaul excluded Ashveil from his private rubbing session. Ashveil sent a second urgent letter to Stonebreak via a civilian courier, bypassing the Gnome relay.

Rowen emerged before dawn and used the navigator's charts to tentatively identify a second map point — a coastal shelf northeast of the Tidefall approaches. He called his first officers' meeting since the descent and told them movement would come within forty-eight hours. He did not name a destination. Fleet morale stabilized on the promise of motion.

Caeris submitted the proclamation text to the High Cleric for review and sent three coordinated letters: a conditional acceptance to Glenhaven, a stand-down acknowledgment to Dur Khadur, and a reassurance note to Lord Halveth. Farrock's formal inquiry about the northern corridor arrived by late afternoon and was noted but deferred. Lady Orryn's answer is expected tomorrow morning.

The council voted narrowly: the prophet's relay does not constitute a written statement. The formal deadline was posted on the Admiral's door — he has until dawn tomorrow. The prophet was informed and given no formal role. The Dur Khadur dispatch and the Hollow Accord letter remain unread on the desk.

Lythara's personal envoy departed for Arkenor before dawn. Velorn's representative followed six hours later. Lythara spent the day refining her offer — shifting from political alliance to a commercial guarantee and intelligence council seat. The Vampire operative ruled out Shadow Court origin for the Admiral's document and transmitted a physical description of the grey-cloaked courier.

active_tensions.factions: Lostfeld — Entity, Ironmaul, and Ashveil (Information Fracture)
active_tensions.description: Varek named Aldric. The case produced Concordance etchings. Ashveil recognizes them and is concealing that recognition from Ironmaul while dispatching a second covert letter. Ironmaul is fortifying his institutional position without knowing the etchings carry specific doctrinal significance. Both actors are now withholding from each other. The entity appears to be communicating and escalating simultaneously.

active_tensions.factions: Twin Cities — Proclamation, Four Pressures, One Day Remaining
active_tensions.description: Lady Orryn answers tomorrow. The High Cleric reviews for twenty-four hours. Glenhaven Wayfarers have been formally accepted and are entering Twin Cities territory. Dur Khadur has signaled it will stand down on Caeris's formal order. Farrock's northern inquiry sits unread in the stack. All threads resolve or fracture within the next twenty-four hours.

active_tensions.factions: Tidefall — Admiral's Final Day, Council Fracture, Unread Documents
active_tensions.description: The Admiral has until dawn tomorrow. The council voted narrowly on the prophet's report. The unread Dur Khadur and Hollow Accord documents on the desk will transfer to the council chair if incapacitation is declared. The grey-cloaked document courier is still unidentified. Everything hinges on whether the Admiral opens his door.

active_tensions.factions: Shadow Court — Two Envoys Racing to Arkenor
active_tensions.description: Lythara's envoy carries intelligence and a commercial offer. Velorn's representative carries a council-record advantage. Arkenor controls three Gnome-pass trade routes and has been profitable during instability — he is a commercial actor, not merely a political one. Both sides may be miscalculating what he wants. The outcome within one to two days reshapes the Court.

seer_state.belief_about_user: The presence beyond the veil is not fully understood.
